moduleDescription:
Contains functionality shared between the httpclient and asynchttpserver modules.

Unstable API.

entries.name: HttpHeaders
entries.code:
HttpHeaders = ref object
  table*: TableRef[string, seq[string]]

entries.name: HttpMethod
entries.code:
HttpMethod = enum
  HttpHead = "HEAD",        ## Asks for the response identical to the one that
                             ## would correspond to a GET request, but without
                             ## the response body.
  HttpGet = "GET",          ## Retrieves the specified resource.
  HttpPost = "POST",        ## Submits data to be processed to the identified
                             ## resource. The data is included in the body of
                             ## the request.
  HttpPut = "PUT",          ## Uploads a representation of the specified
                             ## resource.
  HttpDelete = "DELETE",    ## Deletes the specified resource.
  HttpTrace = "TRACE",      ## Echoes back the received request, so that a
                             ## client
                             ## can see what intermediate servers are adding or
                             ## changing in the request.
  HttpOptions = "OPTIONS",  ## Returns the HTTP methods that the server
                             ## supports for specified address.
  HttpConnect = "CONNECT",  ## Converts the request connection to a transparent
                             ## TCP/IP tunnel, usually used for proxies.
  HttpPatch = "PATCH"        ## Applies partial modifications to a resource.
entries.description: the requested HttpMethod

entries.name: toCaseInsensitive
entries.code:
func toCaseInsensitive(headers: HttpHeaders; s: string): string {.inline,
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: For internal usage only. Do not use.

entries.name: newHttpHeaders
entries.code:
func newHttpHeaders(titleCase = false): HttpHeaders {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns a new HttpHeaders object. if titleCase is set to true, headers are passed to the server in title case (e.g. "Content-Length")

entries.name: newHttpHeaders
entries.description: Returns a new HttpHeaders object from an array. if titleCase is set to true, headers are passed to the server in title case (e.g. "Content-Length")

entries.code:
func `$`(headers: HttpHeaders): string {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

entries.name: clear
entries.code: proc clear(headers: HttpHeaders) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

func `[]`(headers: HttpHeaders; key: string): HttpHeaderValues {.
    raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Returns the values associated with the given key. If the returned values are passed to a procedure expecting a string, the first value is automatically picked. If there are no values associated with the key, an exception is raised.

To access multiple values of a key, use the overloaded [] below or to get all of them access the table field directly.

entries.name: toString
entries.code:
converter toString(values: HttpHeaderValues): string {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

func `[]`(headers: HttpHeaders; key: string; i: int): string {.
    raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the i'th value associated with the given key. If there are no values associated with the key or the i'th value doesn't exist, an exception is raised.

entries.code:
proc `[]=`(headers: HttpHeaders; key, value: string) {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the header entries associated with key to the specified value. Replaces any existing values.

entries.code:
proc `[]=`(headers: HttpHeaders; key: string; value: seq[string]) {.raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the header entries associated with key to the specified list of values. Replaces any existing values. If value is empty, deletes the header entries associated with key.

entries.name: add
entries.code:
proc add(headers: HttpHeaders; key, value: string) {.raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Adds the specified value to the specified key. Appends to any existing values associated with the key.

entries.name: del
entries.code: proc del(headers: HttpHeaders; key: string) {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Deletes the header entries associated with key

entries.name: pairs
entries.code:
iterator pairs(headers: HttpHeaders): tuple[key, value: string] {.raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Yields each key, value pair.

entries.name: contains
entries.code:
func contains(values: HttpHeaderValues; value: string): bool {.raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Determines if value is one of the values inside values. Comparison is performed without case sensitivity.

entries.name: hasKey
entries.code:
func hasKey(headers: HttpHeaders; key: string): bool {.raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

entries.name: getOrDefault
entries.description: Returns the values associated with the given key. If there are no values associated with the key, then default is returned.

entries.name: len
entries.code: func len(headers: HttpHeaders): int {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: parseHeader
entries.code:
func parseHeader(line: string): tuple[key: string, value: seq[string]] {.
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Parses a single raw header HTTP line into key value pairs.

Used by asynchttpserver and httpclient internally and should not be used by you.

entries.code:
func `==`(protocol: tuple[orig: string, major, minor: int]; ver: HttpVersion): bool {.
    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.name: contains
entries.code:
func contains(methods: set[HttpMethod]; x: string): bool {.raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

entries.description: Converts the specified HttpCode into a HTTP status.

entries.code: func `==`(a, b: HttpCode): bool {.borrow, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
